name: Srividhya Sridhar
title: Ph.D. Candidate (Stage II)
bio: I am a Ph.D. candidate in Mechanical Science and Engineering at UIUC, advised by Prof. Shelby Hutchens. My research focuses on mechanical failure of soft polymeric materials—combining experiments such as Y-shaped cutting, needle insertion, and fractography with optical profilometry to relate fracture, friction, and surface morphology. I earned my M.S. in Aerospace Engineering at UIUC (Prof. Huck Beng Chew) before transferring to the Ph.D. program, and my B.E. in Aeronautical Engineering from SNS College of Technology (Anna University), India. I teach and mentor in thermodynamics and solid mechanics, and I care about curriculum development and broadening participation in engineering.
